name: Rental Car Counter — Overbookings, Insurance Decline, Fuel Reversal
description: Navigate the rental-car counter without being upsold: refuse unnecessary insurance, reverse the pre-purchase fuel trap, and handle overbookings without spending an extra $40/day on a 'free upgrade'.
disclaimer: Always confirm your credit-card rental coverage details with the issuer for your specific country and rental type. Some countries (Italy, Ireland, Israel, Jamaica) require local CDW that bypasses credit-card coverage.

context.when_to_use: User is picking up (or returning) a rental car from Hertz, Avis, Enterprise/National/Alamo, Budget, Sixt, Europcar, Dollar, Thrifty, or a local operator. Specifically helpful at airport counters where upsell pressure is highest.
preconditions: User has the reservation confirmation number.
User has their primary credit card (the one that pays — also the one carrying CDW coverage).

User knows whether their credit-card rental coverage is PRIMARY or SECONDARY (Chase Sapphire Reserve = primary in the US for most rentals; Amex Platinum = secondary unless you pre-enroll in Premium Car Rental Protection).
User has a domestic driver's license + IDP if renting abroad in a country that requires it.

principles: Insurance: the most-upsold, least-needed product at the counter. Most US-issued credit cards include CDW. If your card is PRIMARY (Sapphire Reserve, United Club Infinite, USAA), you almost never need the counter's CDW.
Local CDW where required: in Italy, Ireland, Israel, Jamaica, the rental agreement requires CDW that overrides card coverage. Pay it. Elsewhere — decline.

Pre-purchase fuel ('FPO') is a trap unless you're returning empty. The math: you pay for a full tank at a slightly cheaper rate, then return as empty as you dare. If you return with even 1/4 tank, you've paid for fuel you didn't use.
Overbooking is rare but happens. You're entitled to an equivalent or better car at the original rate. 'Free upgrade' usually means same daily rate; an SUV at SUV rate isn't an upgrade.
Status programs skip the counter entirely. Hertz Gold Plus Rewards, Avis Preferred, National Emerald Club Executive — get the keys directly from the lot. Five seconds to enroll for free.
Inspect the car before driving off. Photograph every panel and the dashboard fuel level. Tiny dents become $500 charges if you don't have a timestamp.

phrases.opener: Hi — picking up reservation [confirmation]. I'm declining all optional coverage. I have primary CDW through my credit card. Could we keep the original rate?
phrases.decline_cdw: I'm declining LDW/CDW. My credit card covers it as primary. I'll initial the decline box.
phrases.decline_sli: I'm declining the supplemental liability insurance. My personal auto policy covers liability when I rent.
phrases.decline_pec: I'm declining personal effects coverage. My homeowners/renters policy covers items in the car.
phrases.decline_fpo: I don't need fuel pre-purchase. I'll refuel before return. If your system added it, please remove it now.
phrases.handle_overbook: If the car class I reserved isn't available, can you put me in an equivalent or better class at the same daily rate?
phrases.handle_pressure: Thanks, but I'm set. Can we finalize the keys?
phrases.fuel_reversal_at_return: I returned the car with a full tank. The pre-purchase fuel option was added at check-out. Can you reverse it on this receipt?
phrases.graceful_close_success: Thanks. Can you print or email the final agreement so I can confirm the daily rate and no add-ons before I drive off?
phrases.graceful_close_failure: If you can't waive the [add-on], please print the agreement as-is — I'll dispute the charge with my credit card on return.

tactics.name: Pre-enroll in loyalty before you arrive
tactics.script: Hertz Gold Plus Rewards, Avis Preferred, National Emerald Club, Enterprise Plus, Budget Fastbreak, Sixt Loyalty — all free, all skip the counter. National Executive (free with no spend) lets you pick ANY car in the Executive aisle.

tactics.name: Know your card's CDW terms
tactics.script: PRIMARY US CDW cards: Chase Sapphire Reserve, Chase United Club Infinite, USAA, Amex Platinum (only when you pre-enroll in 'Premium Car Rental Protection' — $12.25/rental). Most other cards are SECONDARY = pay your personal auto first, card picks up remainder. Excluded categories on most cards: exotic, vintage (10+ yrs), trucks/cargo vans, rentals longer than 31 days. Always read the benefits guide.

tactics.name: Decline every add-on by name
tactics.script: LDW/CDW, SLI (supplemental liability), PAI (personal accident), PEC (personal effects), roadside assistance plus, fuel pre-purchase. Decline each. Initial the boxes. Calm and friendly, not adversarial.

tactics.name: Refuse a 'free upgrade' that isn't free
tactics.when: Agent offers an SUV / premium because your class is unavailable.
tactics.script: Ask: 'At what daily rate? Same as my reservation?' If no — that's not an upgrade, that's an upsell. Per industry norms, you're owed an equivalent or better car at the ORIGINAL rate when your class is unavailable.

tactics.name: Photograph every panel, plus dash + odometer
tactics.script: Walk around the car. Photograph each side, both bumpers, roof, hood, all four wheels, the dashboard showing fuel level and odometer, and any existing damage marked or unmarked. Time-stamped photos beat any dispute later.

tactics.name: Reverse FPO at return if accidentally added
tactics.when: Returning with full tank but the system shows FPO on receipt.
tactics.script: Show the agent the fuel receipt and the receipt's FPO line. They can remove it on the spot 90% of the time. If not, dispute with credit card.

tactics.name: Foreign rental: prepay local CDW
tactics.when: Italy, Ireland, Jamaica, Israel, parts of Eastern Europe.
tactics.script: Use Auto Europe, AutoSlash, or the rental brand's site with local CDW INCLUDED in the booking. The counter will still try to upsell 'Super CDW'; usually you can decline that if you have card coverage.

boundaries: Do NOT decline local CDW where it's legally required (Italy is the most common trap).
Do NOT drive an exotic/luxury class assuming card coverage applies — check the card's exclusions.
Do NOT skip the pre-rental walkaround. Photos prevent disputes.
Do NOT lie about who's driving — adding a driver costs $0-$15/day and matters for insurance.
Do NOT use the rental for prohibited activities (off-road, ride-sharing, towing) — voids both rental and card coverage.
success_signals: Agent prints the agreement with no add-ons and the original daily rate.
Walkaround done and photographed without rush.
Equivalent or better car offered at the original rate when overbooked.
Final receipt at return matches the agreement with no surprise charges.
failure_signals: Agent insists you 'must' take CDW (false in the US absent specific exclusions).
FPO is added despite your decline.
Daily rate at counter is higher than the reservation rate without explanation.
Car has unmarked damage and agent refuses to mark it.

exit_strategy.on_success: Drive off with the agreement saved and photos timestamped. Refuel within 10 minutes of return and keep the receipt.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Pay under protest, finalize the agreement, and dispute the unauthorized add-ons with the credit-card issuer using your initialed decline boxes as evidence.
follow_ups: On return, photograph the car again with the fuel needle and odometer.
If charged for damage you didn't cause, file a card dispute within 60 days with your photos.
If the rental was for a corporate trip, submit the receipt promptly — many corp T&E systems reject if uploaded >30 days late.

required_user_info: Credit card used to book + whether its CDW is PRIMARY or SECONDARY
Whether the rental is in a country requiring local CDW
